ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Indonesian green chilli sambal (sambal cabe ijo). FOODON_03311737 (chili pepper sauce) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Chilli sauce and similar products) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for sambal cabe ijo in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for sambal cabe ijo or Indonesian green chilli sambal specifically.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical condiment serving of sambal cabe ijo is approximately 30 g (about 2 tablespoons), served alongside martabak, rice dishes, or grilled meats.

nutrient_source.title: Chili sauce, green, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods; supplemented with FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table data for sambal-type condiments
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for sambal cabe ijo exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for raw green chilli pepper sauce / green chilli paste (FDC ID 170931 and related entries) and cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA composition data for chilli-based condiments. The product is described as a fresh paste of green chillies with shallots, garlic, salt, lime juice, and oil. Macros reflect a blended fresh green chilli condiment with moderate oil content.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of chilli and chilli-based condiments — analogous estimate based on raw green chilli pepper data; University of Sydney GI Database reference for non-starchy vegetables and chilli products
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for sambal cabe ijo in the University of Sydney GI Database. Green chillies are non-starchy vegetables with very low carbohydrate content. The GI is estimated at 15 (low) by analogy with raw green chilli pepper and similar non-starchy vegetable condiments documented in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 30 g serving = (15 × 7.2 × 0.30) / 100 ≈ 0.3, which is negligible.

hypertension.guidance: Sambal cabe ijo contains approximately 480 mg sodium per 100 g; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 1–2 teaspoons (10–15 g) per meal to keep sodium contribution below 75 mg per serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index (~15) and low carbohydrate content (~7.2 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Sambal cabe ijo has a very low glycemic index of approximately 15 and a negligible glycemic load per typical serving, making it a suitable condiment for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes when used in standard condiment quantities.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.8 g per 100 g), predominantly unsaturated fat profile, and presence of capsaicin and vitamin C
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Sambal cabe ijo has a favourable fat profile with low saturated fat and a predominance of monounsaturated fat; capsaicin in green chillies may support vascular health, though sodium content warrants moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sambal Cabe Ijo (Green Chilli Sambal) is a traditional Indonesian condiment paste made primarily from fresh green chillies (cabe ijo), shallots, garlic, salt, lime juice, and a small amount of cooking oil. The paste is typically ground or blended to a coarse or smooth consistency and may be served raw or lightly cooked. It has a bright, fresh, herbaceous flavour with pronounced heat and a slight tanginess from lime. The texture ranges from chunky to smooth depending on preparation. Sambal cabe ijo is a staple accompaniment in Indonesian cuisine, commonly served alongside martabak (stuffed savoury pancake), nasi padang, grilled fish, and rice dishes. Nutritionally, it is low in calories (~82 kcal per 100 g), low in carbohydrates (~7.2 g per 100 g), and provides a moderate amount of dietary fibre (~2.1 g per 100 g) and vitamin C (~72 mg per 100 g). The glycemic index is very low (~15), making it suitable for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g) is the primary clinical consideration, and portion control is advised for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The product sold at Smile Martabak in Singapore is described as made from scratch with green chillies imported from Indonesia.
common_user_queries: How many calories in sambal cabe ijo?
Is sambal cabe ijo suitable for diabetics?
What is the difference between sambal cabe ijo and sambal merah?

Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for raw green chilli pepper sauce and cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA data for chilli-based condiments. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central (FDC) data for raw green chilli pepper sauce and blended chilli condiments, cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition data for sambal-type products. No exact database entry for sambal cabe ijo was found in any primary regional source. The product is described by the restaurant (Smile Martabak, Funan, Singapore) as made from scratch with green chillies imported from Indonesia. Sodium value (~480 mg per 100 g) reflects typical added salt in sambal preparations and is an estimate; actual sodium may vary by recipe. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — green chilli paste with vegetable oil contains no industrially produced trans fats). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero — this is a plant-based condiment with no animal-derived ingredients). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero — green chilli paste is not a source of vitamin D). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero — plant-based condiment with no animal-derived ingredients). All -1 sentinel values in per_100g from the template have been populated with adapted estimates from the approved source. GI set to 15 (low) by analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for non-starchy vegetable chilli condiments. Glycemic load per 30 g serving is approximately 0.3, which is negligible. Indonesian cuisine: mandatory Indonesian local name 'Sambal Cabe Ijo' is present.
